data.title: Cumulus Media’s Chapter 11 Restructuring Risks Ownership Change, Threatening Valuable NOL Tax Assets
data.description:
Cumulus Media, Inc. (CMLSQ) has disclosed a new risk, in the Debt & Financing category.

Easter Sale - 70% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Cumulus Media, Inc. faces the risk that equity transfers and new issuances tied to its Chapter 11 proceedings trigger an “ownership change” under Section 382 of the Internal Revenue Code. Such a change could severely limit its ability to use existing NOL carryforwards and other tax attributes, potentially increasing future cash tax payments and straining its financial position.

Because the company has already undergone prior ownership changes and expects another upon implementation of its Plan, the cumulative impact may materially constrain the remaining value of its tax attributes. In a downside scenario, some or all NOLs could be permanently lost, removing a key tool for offsetting future taxable income and adding volatility to reported results and cash flows.

Overall, Wall Street has a Hold consensus rating on CMLSQ stock based on 1 Hold.

To learn more about Cumulus Media, Inc.’s risk factors, click here.
